last_digest.title: 7-Eleven confirms data breach claimed by the ShinyHunters gang
last_digest.rss_summary: Convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven confirmed that its systems were breached in a cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion group last month. [...]

last_digest.title: Microsoft disrupts cybercrime service that abused software verification systems en masse
last_digest.rss_summary:
Fox Tempest, a financially-motivated threat group, allowed ransomware operators and other cybercriminals to slip malware-laced software past security controls.
The post Microsoft disrupts cybercrime service that abused software verification systems en masse appeared first on CyberScoop.

last_digest.title: New Shai-Hulud malware wave compromises 600 npm packages
last_digest.rss_summary: Threat actors earlier today published more than 600 malicious packages to the Node Package Manager (npm) index as part of a new Shai-Hulud supply-chain campaign. [...]

last_digest.title: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector
last_digest.rss_summary:
Verizon’s 2026 DBIR finds vulnerability exploitation has overtaken credential abuse as the leading breach vector, as AI accelerates attacks, patching delays worsen, and ransomware and third-party compromises continue to surge.
The post Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector appeared first on SecurityWeek.

last_digest.title: Huawei zero-day attack behind last year’s crash of Luxembourg's entire telecoms network
last_digest.rss_summary: There is no evidence that the incident has recurred, but the flaw remains unexplained and has not been publicly acknowledged by the company.

last_week_digest.title: Poor security left hackers inside water company network for nearly two years
last_week_digest.tldr: The UK Information Commissioner’s Office fined South Staffordshire Water £963,900 after hackers remained undetected within their network for 20 months, exposing personal data of 633,887 individuals. This incident, caused by weak controls and outdated software, resulted in 4.1 terabytes of stolen data. It signals a critical industry shift, highlighting that regulators now demand proactive monitoring and robust vulnerability management from essential infrastructure providers to protect sensitive customer information.

last_week_digest.title: Google warns artificial intelligence is accelerating cyberattacks and zero-day exploits
last_week_digest.tldr: Google reports that cybercriminals are now leveraging artificial intelligence to automate vulnerability discovery and accelerate exploit development, significantly shortening the window for defensive patching. By integrating AI into the full attack lifecycle, threat actors are successfully targeting cloud environments and software supply chains at scale. This shift signals a new era of autonomous cybercrime, where reduced technical barriers enable more sophisticated, rapid, and adaptive global security threats.

last_week_digest.title: UK water company allowed hackers to lurk undetected for nearly two years, regulator finds
last_week_digest.tldr: South Staffordshire Water faces a £963,900 fine after failing to detect Cl0p ransomware actors lurking within its network for nearly two years. The breach exposed personal data of 633,887 individuals due to severe security lapses, including unpatched systems and inadequate monitoring. This incident signals a critical shift in regulatory expectations, highlighting that reactive security postures are insufficient for essential infrastructure providers managing sensitive public data and operations.

last_week_digest.title: Google researchers uncover criminal zero-day exploit likely built with AI
last_week_digest.tldr: Google researchers recently identified a zero-day exploit targeting open-source administration tools, revealing how criminal groups leverage AI to accelerate vulnerability discovery and code obfuscation. This shift signals a broader industry trend where automated development tools enable rapid, large-scale deployment of sophisticated malware, including autonomous Android backdoors. For global businesses, these advancements necessitate enhanced detection strategies, as AI-generated code increasingly obscures malicious activity, complicating traditional security response efforts.

last_week_digest.title: Build Application Firewalls Aim to Stop the Next Supply Chain Attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Supply chain vulnerabilities remain a critical threat, with recent incidents exposing thousands of downstream users to malicious code injections. By shifting focus from static code scanning to inspecting runtime behaviour within build pipelines, Build Application Firewalls provide a necessary layer of defence against sophisticated tampering. This transition signals an industry-wide move towards securing the software development lifecycle, prioritising operational integrity to prevent catastrophic financial and reputational losses globally.

last_week_digest.title: Škoda warns of customer data breach after online shop hack
last_week_digest.tldr: Škoda Auto has confirmed a data breach involving its online shop, where attackers exploited a software vulnerability to access customer names, contact details, and hashed passwords. While financial data remained secure, the incident highlights the persistent risk to automotive e-commerce platforms. This breach, following recent attacks on Jaguar Land Rover and Renault, signals an industry-wide vulnerability where supply chain and digital retail systems increasingly serve as primary targets for cybercriminals.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers accessed BWH Hotels reservation system for months
last_week_digest.tldr: BWH Hotels confirmed that threat actors accessed reservation systems for over six months, exposing the names, contact details, and stay information of guests across its 4,000 global properties. While financial data remained secure, the prolonged unauthorised access highlights significant vulnerabilities in hospitality infrastructure. This incident signals an urgent industry-wide need for enhanced monitoring of reservation platforms, as attackers increasingly exploit long-term access to facilitate sophisticated, targeted phishing campaigns.

last_week_digest.title: West Pharmaceutical warns of ransomware attack impacting business operations
last_week_digest.tldr: West Pharmaceutical Services recently suffered a significant ransomware attack that compromised critical manufacturing, shipping, and receiving systems across its global network. The breach, impacting a company with over 10,000 employees and $3 billion in sales, highlights the escalating vulnerability of healthcare supply chains. This incident signals a broader industry trend where cybercriminals increasingly target essential medical infrastructure, threatening both operational continuity and the safety of global patient care.

last_week_digest.title: WannaCry, the ransomware attack that changed the history of cybersecurity
last_week_digest.tldr: The 2017 WannaCry ransomware attack infected over 200,000 systems across 150 countries, causing severe operational paralysis for critical infrastructure, including British hospitals. By weaponising leaked intelligence-grade exploits, the worm demonstrated the catastrophic risks of unpatched legacy software. This event fundamentally shifted global cybersecurity, signalling that state-sponsored tools can easily be repurposed by criminals, forcing organisations to prioritise rapid patch management and network segmentation to mitigate systemic digital threats.

last_week_digest.title: Exaforce Raises $125 Million for Agentic SOC Platform
last_week_digest.tldr: Exaforce has secured $125 million in fresh funding, bringing its total capital raised to $200 million to accelerate the development of agentic security operations centre platforms. This significant investment signals a broader industry shift towards autonomous, AI-driven threat detection and response capabilities. For global enterprises, this trend highlights a move to automate complex security workflows, aiming to reduce operational overhead while addressing critical talent shortages worldwide.

last_week_digest.title: Signal responds to phishing attacks with new in-app security warnings
last_week_digest.tldr: Signal is implementing enhanced in-app security warnings following sophisticated phishing campaigns that exploited its linked devices feature. Intelligence agencies, including the FBI and CISA, identified Russian-linked actors targeting users, prompting urgent intervention to prevent account compromises. These incidents highlight the growing operational risk of social engineering against encrypted communication platforms, signalling that even secure services must now prioritise user-facing authentication safeguards to mitigate widespread business and intelligence threats.

last_week_digest.title: Iranian hackers targeted major South Korean electronics maker
last_week_digest.tldr: The Iran-linked group MuddyWater recently executed a sophisticated espionage campaign against nine global entities, including a major South Korean electronics manufacturer. By abusing legitimate software binaries for one week, attackers conducted extensive intellectual property theft and reconnaissance. This breach signals a concerning industry shift toward quieter, implant-driven operations that leverage trusted tools to evade detection, highlighting the critical need for advanced behavioural monitoring across international supply chains.

last_week_digest.title: China's 'FamousSparrow' APT Nests in South Caucasus Energy Firm
last_week_digest.tldr: The China-linked FamousSparrow group has infiltrated an Azerbaijani oil and gas firm, marking a strategic expansion into the South Caucasus energy corridor. By employing sophisticated DLL sideloading techniques, the actors bypassed traditional defences to conduct espionage between December and February. This incursion signals a shift in global cyber operations, as Chinese-aligned groups increasingly challenge Russian spheres of influence to monitor critical infrastructure vital to European energy security.

last_week_digest.title: Mystery Microsoft bug leaker keeps the zero-days coming
last_week_digest.tldr: An anonymous researcher has disclosed five Microsoft zero-day vulnerabilities this year, including the recent YellowKey and GreenPlasma exploits. YellowKey allows attackers with physical access to bypass BitLocker encryption, effectively turning stolen hardware into significant data breach risks for global enterprises. These revelations signal a growing trend of disgruntled insiders weaponising technical knowledge, forcing organisations to urgently reassess physical security protocols and rely less on single-layer encryption defences.

last_week_digest.title: Foxconn Confirms North American Factories Hit by Cyberattack
last_week_digest.tldr: Foxconn has confirmed a cyberattack on its North American facilities, with the Nitrogen ransomware group claiming responsibility for the theft of eight terabytes of confidential corporate data. This breach disrupts critical manufacturing operations and highlights the escalating vulnerability of global supply chains to sophisticated extortion tactics. The incident serves as a stark signal that large-scale industrial enterprises remain primary targets, necessitating urgent improvements in cross-border digital resilience.

last_week_digest.title: 'FrostyNeighbor' APT Carefully Targets Govt Orgs in Poland, Ukraine
last_week_digest.tldr: The Belarussian threat group FrostyNeighbor is conducting a highly targeted espionage campaign against government and military organisations in Poland and Ukraine. By employing sophisticated server-side victim validation and spear-phishing PDFs, attackers manually verify targets before deploying Cobalt Strike. This evolution signals a shift toward more precise, resource-intensive operations, indicating that state-sponsored actors are increasingly prioritising quality over quantity to bypass traditional security defences and maintain long-term access.

last_week_digest.title: China-Linked Hackers Deploy New TencShell Malware Against Global Manufacturer
last_week_digest.tldr: China-linked actors recently deployed a novel TencShell malware implant against an Indian branch of a global manufacturer. By repurposing the open-source Rshell framework to mimic legitimate Tencent traffic, attackers attempted to gain persistent remote command execution and system access. This incident signals a broader industry shift where sophisticated threat groups increasingly leverage adaptable, low-cost offensive tooling to bypass security defences and blend malicious activity into standard enterprise traffic.

last_week_digest.title: Major tech manufacturer Foxconn confirms cyberattack hit North American factories
last_week_digest.tldr: Electronics manufacturer Foxconn recently suffered a cyberattack by the Nitrogen ransomware group, which claims to have stolen eight terabytes of data across 11 million files, including sensitive intellectual property from major global tech partners. This breach disrupted North American production, highlighting the critical vulnerability of global supply chains. The incident signals a shift toward threat actors targeting high-value manufacturing hubs to exert maximum pressure for ransom payments.

last_week_digest.title: Akamai to Acquire AI and Browser Security Firm LayerX for $205 Million
last_week_digest.tldr: Akamai Technologies has announced the acquisition of browser security startup LayerX for $205 million, a strategic move designed to bolster its Zero Trust portfolio. By integrating AI-driven protection directly into the browser, the firm aims to mitigate rising web-based threats and credential theft. This consolidation signals a broader industry shift towards securing the endpoint environment, reflecting the critical need for robust, identity-centric defences within modern distributed enterprises.

last_week_digest.title: OpenAI asks macOS users to update after TanStack npm supply chain attack
last_week_digest.tldr: OpenAI has mandated macOS application updates by June 12 after a supply chain attack compromised its signing keys. The breach, stemming from malicious TanStack npm packages, affected two employee devices and internal source code repositories. While no customer data was stolen, the incident highlights systemic risks within open-source ecosystems, where 84 compromised packages with 12 million weekly downloads demonstrate the severe potential for widespread downstream operational disruption.

last_week_digest.title: SECURITY AFFAIRS MALWARE NEWSLETTER ROUND 97
last_week_digest.tldr: Recent malware campaigns, including the Mini Shai-Hulud npm supply chain attack and vulnerabilities affecting 40,000 WooCommerce checkouts, highlight escalating risks to global digital infrastructure. These incidents demonstrate how threat actors exploit trusted software repositories and e-commerce platforms to deploy backdoors and steal credentials. Such developments signal a shift toward sophisticated, automated distribution methods, forcing organisations to prioritise robust supply chain security and proactive vulnerability management to mitigate operational disruptions.

last_week_digest.title: Russian APT Turla builds long-term access tool with Kazuar Botnet evolution
last_week_digest.tldr: The Russian-linked APT group Turla has evolved its Kazuar malware into a sophisticated peer-to-peer botnet, enabling persistent, covert espionage against diplomatic and government entities globally. By utilising modular architecture to minimise external network signatures, the group maintains long-term access while complicating detection efforts. This development signals a strategic shift toward highly resilient, stealth-focused infrastructure, forcing organisations to prioritise behavioural monitoring over traditional signature-based defences to counter state-sponsored threats.

last_week_digest.title: Microsoft Exchange, Windows 11 hacked on second day of Pwn2Own
last_week_digest.tldr: Security researchers at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 successfully exploited 15 zero-day vulnerabilities across Windows 11, Microsoft Exchange, and Red Hat Enterprise Linux, securing $385,750 in awards. These demonstrations of arbitrary code execution highlight significant risks within enterprise environments and emerging AI tools. This event signals a persistent industry challenge, as vendors face a critical 90-day window to patch these flaws before they are potentially weaponised against global business infrastructure.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers Earn $1.3 Million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026
last_week_digest.tldr: Security researchers earned $1.3 million at Pwn2Own Berlin 2026 by demonstrating critical vulnerabilities across essential enterprise infrastructure, including Windows, Linux, VMware, Nvidia, and various AI products. These findings highlight significant operational risks for global organisations relying on these platforms, as unpatched flaws could lead to widespread system compromise. The event signals a growing industry focus on securing complex AI-driven ecosystems against sophisticated exploits targeting modern digital supply chains.

last_week_digest.title: More than $10 million stolen from crypto platform THORChain
last_week_digest.tldr: Cryptocurrency platform THORChain recently suffered a $10.7 million theft after a vault compromise, forcing a temporary suspension of trading activities. While protocol-owned funds were targeted, user assets remained secure due to automated network safeguards. This incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of decentralised finance infrastructures, signalling a broader industry trend where sophisticated cyberattacks continue to threaten global digital asset stability, necessitating enhanced threat intelligence and robust security protocols.

last_week_digest.title: Grafana confirms GitHub token breach cybercrime group claims the attack
last_week_digest.tldr: Grafana Labs recently confirmed a security breach involving a compromised GitHub token, allowing the Coinbase Cartel extortion group to access internal source code. While the company reported no impact on customer data or operational systems, the incident highlights the growing threat to software supply chains. This event signals that developers’ environments are now primary targets for extortionists, necessitating stricter token management and robust, phishing-resistant authentication protocols globally.

last_week_digest.title: Shai-Hulud worm copycats emerge after source code leak
last_week_digest.tldr: Following the leak of the Shai-Hulud worm’s source code, threat actors have launched multiple copycat attacks targeting NPM developers. Four malicious packages, including one disguised as a utility, accumulated over 2,600 weekly downloads before detection. This incident signals a dangerous shift where sophisticated supply chain exploits are now accessible to less skilled attackers, significantly increasing the risk of credential theft and botnet recruitment across global software ecosystems.

last_week_digest.title: Leaked Shai-Hulud malware fuels new npm infostealer campaign
last_week_digest.tldr: Threat actors have weaponised leaked Shai-Hulud source code to launch a new npm supply-chain campaign, deploying four malicious packages that achieved 2,678 downloads. By employing typosquatting against popular libraries, attackers successfully targeted developer credentials, cryptocurrency wallets, and cloud configurations. This incident highlights the growing risk of commoditised malware, signalling that software supply chains remain highly vulnerable to rapid exploitation as threat actors leverage public code leaks.

last_week_digest.title: Millions Impacted Across Several US Healthcare Data Breaches
last_week_digest.tldr: Recent additions to the HHS tracker confirm that multiple healthcare organisations have suffered significant data breaches, collectively compromising the sensitive personal information of millions of patients. These incidents disrupt critical clinical operations and erode public trust, highlighting the severe operational risks posed by inadequate digital defences. This trend signals an urgent industry-wide requirement for enhanced cybersecurity investment to protect patient privacy and ensure the continuity of essential services.

last_week_digest.title: TanStack weighs invitation-only pull requests after supply chain attack
last_week_digest.tldr: The TanStack project is considering restricting pull requests to invitation-only following a supply chain attack where the Shai-Hulud worm poisoned its GitHub Actions cache. This breach forced the team to overhaul CI pipelines and disable vulnerable features. The incident highlights the growing tension between maintaining open-source collaboration and securing critical development infrastructure, signalling a shift toward more restrictive security models to mitigate persistent, automated supply chain threats.

last_week_digest.title: 7-Eleven confirms data breach claimed by the ShinyHunters gang
last_week_digest.tldr: Convenience store chain giant 7-Eleven confirmed that its systems were breached in a cyberattack claimed by the ShinyHunters extortion group last month. [...]

last_week_digest.title: Microsoft disrupts cybercrime service that abused software verification systems en masse
last_week_digest.tldr: Microsoft has dismantled a cybercrime operation that generated over 1,000 fraudulent code-signing certificates, enabling threat actors to bypass security controls by masquerading malware as legitimate software. By charging up to $9,500 per certificate, the group facilitated ransomware attacks across global sectors including finance and healthcare. This disruption highlights a dangerous industry shift where attackers now exploit the foundational trust systems that organisations rely upon to verify digital authenticity.

last_week_digest.title: New Shai-Hulud malware wave compromises 600 npm packages
last_week_digest.tldr: The Shai-Hulud malware campaign recently compromised 639 versions across 323 npm packages, including popular libraries like @antv/g2. By hijacking maintainer accounts, attackers exfiltrated sensitive CI/CD and cloud credentials, creating over 2,900 rogue GitHub repositories. This incident highlights the critical vulnerability of dormant software dependencies, signalling a shift towards sophisticated supply-chain attacks that bypass traditional network security by leveraging encrypted peer-to-peer protocols to exfiltrate stolen corporate data.

last_week_digest.title: Verizon DBIR 2026: Vulnerability Exploitation Overtakes Credential Theft as Top Breach Vector
last_week_digest.tldr: The 2026 Verizon Data Breach Investigations Report reveals that vulnerability exploitation has officially surpassed credential theft as the primary vector for global cyberattacks. Driven by AI-accelerated threats and persistent patching delays, this shift forces organisations to rethink their defensive priorities. As ransomware and third-party compromises surge, businesses must recognise that traditional identity-focused security is no longer sufficient, signalling a critical need for more proactive, rapid vulnerability management strategies.

last_week_digest.title: Huawei zero-day attack behind last year’s crash of Luxembourg's entire telecoms network
last_week_digest.tldr: A sophisticated zero-day vulnerability in Huawei enterprise routers caused a nationwide telecommunications outage in Luxembourg, disabling critical mobile, landline, and emergency services for over three hours. The incident, triggered by malformed network traffic, highlights the systemic risks posed by undocumented software flaws in core infrastructure. This event signals a growing industry challenge where passive transit traffic can inadvertently crash national networks, necessitating greater transparency regarding vendor hardware vulnerabilities.
